codeReview.summary: High-quality audit of memory.rs — the highest-risk unaudited kernel file (19 unsafe blocks + 1 unsafe impl). The AUDIT comment is accurate and comprehensive, covering break-before-make, W^X enforcement, TLB sequences, and the neighbor-based attribute recovery invariant. All 19 unsafe blocks have accurate SAFETY comments that correctly identify the invariants they rely on. The init() BBM skip is well-justified (single-core boot context). 32 model-based tests faithfully replicate the kernel's pure logic (address translation, W^X attribute computation, L3 entry construction, block attribute extraction, neighbor index, break-before-make sequencing, index extraction, constant invariants). Test models were verified against kernel source — all masks, constants, and logic match exactly. No bugs found is a credible conclusion given the thorough checklist application.

issues.description: AUDIT comment says '19 unsafe blocks + 1 unsafe impl' but the file also contains the `unsafe impl Sync for SyncPageTable` at line 83 which IS the 1 unsafe impl. Count is correct. However, the AUDIT comment could explicitly name the unsafe impl (Sync for SyncPageTable) for future auditors searching for it.

Test `l3_index_extraction` uses literal `0x4021_FF000` (note extra F) — this is 0x4_021F_F000 which is outside the expected 2MB block at 0x4020_0000. The test still passes because it only checks bits [20:12] which happen to be 0x1FF = 511, but the PA value is misleading (it's ~16GB, not within a 2MB block at 0x4020_0000). Should be `0x4021_F000` for page 511 of the block starting at 0x4020_0000. This is a cosmetic issue — the test logic is correct because only the relevant index bits are checked.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the model-based TDD pattern correctly — all 32 tests duplicate pure kernel logic into host-side test models. This pattern is now well-established across M1-M4 and could be documented more prominently in AGENTS.md as the standard approach for kernel audit test writing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/test/tests/memory.rs duplicates virt_to_phys, phys_to_virt, compute_wx_attrs, build_l3_entry, extract_block_attrs, build_replicated_l3_entry, neighbor_index — all verified to match kernel source exactly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The audit documents an important architectural invariant: guard pages are never adjacent because the buddy allocator always places them at the first page of a >=2-page block. This invariant is critical for the soundness of clear_kernel_guard_page's neighbor-based attribute recovery but is not documented anywhere except the AUDIT comment. Should be in architecture.md or a safety map.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: memory.rs AUDIT comment line 7: 'guard is always first page of >=2-page block'. clear_kernel_guard_page relies on neighbor_idx always pointing to a valid mapped page.

summary: Pass. The audit of memory.rs is thorough and correct. All 19 unsafe blocks + 1 unsafe impl have accurate SAFETY comments. The AUDIT marker is comprehensive. 32 model-based tests cover all pure logic paths including address translation, W^X enforcement, L3 entry construction, block attribute extraction, break-before-make sequencing, and index extraction. Test models faithfully replicate kernel logic. The only non-blocking issue is a misleading PA literal in one test (cosmetic — test logic is correct). The init() BBM skip observation is well-documented and correctly justified. No bugs found is a credible conclusion. 1006 tests pass, kernel builds clean.
